locked_facts: Beat frequency = |f₁ - f₂| (absolute difference, NOT sum)
Beats arise from periodic constructive and destructive interference
Loudness varies periodically at beat frequency
truth_statements: Beat frequency equals the absolute difference of the two frequencies
Beats are produced when two sound waves of nearly equal frequencies superpose
The intensity of the resultant wave varies periodically
The number of beats per second equals the difference in frequencies
Beats are used to tune musical instruments

aha_moment.what_to_show: Resultant wave with clear amplitude envelope. Count the beats per second — matches |f₁ - f₂|, NOT f₁ + f₂. Beat envelope drawn in yellow over the green resultant.
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Yellow envelope clearly shows beat period. Label: 'Beat freq = |f₁-f₂| = 2 Hz'. The count of maxima per second equals the difference, not the sum.

simulation_states.label: Wave 1 alone — constant amplitude
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Single sound wave at frequency f₁. Constant amplitude, no variation in loudness.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: A single-frequency wave has constant amplitude. No beats with just one frequency.

simulation_states.label: Two waves — slightly different frequencies
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Two waves shown: f₁ and f₂ (slightly different). Both individual waves have constant amplitude.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: Two waves of nearly equal but not identical frequencies. What happens when they combine?

simulation_states.label: Beats pattern — the aha moment
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Resultant wave with periodically varying amplitude (loud-soft-loud pattern). Beat envelope in yellow. Beat frequency = |f₁-f₂| labelled clearly.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: The resultant amplitude varies periodically. Beat frequency = |f₁-f₂| (DIFFERENCE, not sum). Count the maxima to verify.

simulation_states.label: Interactive — change f₂
simulation_states.what_student_sees: Slider to adjust f₂. As f₂ approaches f₁, beats slow down (longer period). As f₂ moves away, beats speed up.
simulation_states.what_to_emphasize: When f₁ = f₂: no beats (constant amplitude). Larger |f₁-f₂|: faster beats. The formula is always beat freq = |f₁-f₂|.

common_misconceptions.student_belief: Beat frequency equals the sum of two frequencies (f₁ + f₂)
common_misconceptions.actual_physics: Beat frequency equals the absolute DIFFERENCE |f₁ - f₂|, not the sum. The sum (f₁+f₂)/2 is the carrier frequency of the resultant wave.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show resultant with beat envelope. Count maxima per second = |f₁-f₂|. Display both |f₁-f₂| and f₁+f₂ values to show they are different.
